etymology.description: 師(師) (shī, "master; teacher") originally meant "army commander", evoking an image of a leading figure with authority and wisdom. 傅(傅) (fù, "tutor; assist") shows an idea of guidance and support, often in the role of a knowledgeable mentor.
etymology.interpretation: 師(師) (shī, "master; teacher") suggests authority, excellent skills, and leadership, connecting to the role of experts or skilled individuals as masters of their craft. 傅(傅) (fù, "tutor; assist") conveys the idea of teaching and providing support, implying mastery through tutelage or craftsmanship. Together, 师傅(師傅) (shīfu) describes someone both highly skilled and capable of instructing others, whether in education or technical expertise, thus capturing the dual aspects of mastery and mentorship.

examples.chinese: 这位师傅(這位師傅)是一位非常有经验的木匠(是一位非常有經驗的木匠)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè wèi shīfu shì yī wèi fēicháng yǒu jīngyàn de mùjiàng.
examples.english: This master is a very experienced carpenter.

examples.chinese: 汽车坏了(汽車壞了)，我们需要找一个修理的师傅(我們需要找一個修理的師傅)。
examples.pinyin: Qìchē huài le, wǒmen xūyào zhǎo yī gè xiūlǐ de shīfu.
examples.english: The car is broken, we need to find a repair master.

examples.chinese: 餐厅的师傅(餐廳的師傅)做的炒饭非常好吃(做的炒飯非常好吃)。
examples.pinyin: Cāntīng de shīfu zuò de chǎofàn fēicháng hàochī.
examples.english: The restaurant's cook makes very tasty fried rice.
